sources.title: UBS Reiterates Neutral Rating on Blue Owl Capital (OWL)
sources.excerpt: Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:[OWL](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OWL)) is one of the [**10 Best Stocks to Buy Under $10**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/10-best-stocks-to-buy- under-10-1782697/). On June 11, UBS reaffirmed its Neutral rating on Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) with a price target of $9.50 on the stock. The research firm's bull-bear analysis suggests that while some upside potential exists for Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL), the probability does not favor it because of the uncertainty around the reacceleration of private credit growth and business development companies. UBS Reiterates Neutral Rating on Blue Owl Capital (OWL) According to UBS, the shares are expected to remain range-bound. The firm believes that bear points continue to outnumber and outweigh bull points. UBS said that turning constructive on Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) would require confidence in a sustainable low-to-mid-teens fee-related earnings compound annual growth rate. The firm sees this as a challenge that could be difficult for the company to clear through 2028. After a decline from its peak, the stock trades at around 9.9 times UBS's 2027 distributable EPS estimate and 9.4 times market estimates. The research firm noted that in recent weeks, pressure on Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) has eased a little and there has been a modest uptick in interest, though with low conviction. Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) is a leading alternative asset management company that operates through three business lines, namely credit, real assets, and GP Strategic Capital. It provides private capital solutions to businesses and offers differentiated alternative investment opportunities to institutional investors, individual investors, and insurance companies. While we acknowledge th

analysis:
- **External commentary:** UBS reiterated a Neutral rating on OWL with a $9.50 price target, stating that bear points still outnumber bull points and that a sustainable low-to-mid-teens fee-related earnings CAGR through 2028 is a difficult challenge [3]. Separately, unconfirmed market chatter indicates Blue Owl is in advanced talks to buy a 5–10% minority stake in the NBA’s Cleveland Cavaliers [1][2]; the full details are behind a paywall and the company’s response is not publicly available in the excerpts.

- **Why it can work:** The stock trades at a forward P/E of 9.58 and a PEG of 0.14, suggesting low valuation relative to expected earnings growth. Short interest is elevated at 20.6% of float, which could fuel a squeeze if positive catalysts emerge. The potential Cavaliers stake, if confirmed and accretive, could signal new alternative investment verticals and broaden OWL’s asset base.

- **What can break it:** UBS explicitly cites uncertainty around reacceleration of private credit growth and BDC performance as key headwinds [3]. The stock is down 59.3% from its 52-week high and has underperformed its sector by 20.9% in the past month. The trailing P/E of 78.5 implies recent earnings are depressed, and the negative sentiment score (-0.6) suggests weak market confidence. The Cavaliers deal is unconfirmed and its financial impact is unknown.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for official confirmation of the Cavaliers stake and its terms, including purchase price and expected return. Monitor quarterly fee-related earnings growth and any guidance from management on achieving low-to-mid-teens CAGR. A sustained drop in short interest or a positive revision from UBS or other analysts would be supportive. Also watch for improvement in the 1-month peer lag (currently -20.9%).

- **Bottom line:** OWL is a deeply lagging asset manager with high short interest and a low forward P/E, but the fundamental case for a turn is unproven. The only qualitative catalyst is an unconfirmed minority sports stake, and the leading sell-side analyst sees more bear than bull factors through 2028. Without verified positive earnings momentum or a confirmed catalyst, the long setup lacks sufficient qualitative support.

sources.title: Is Global Payments Inc. (GPN) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
sources.excerpt: **Is GPN a good stock to buy?** We came across a [bullish thesis ](https://mispricingdesk.substack.com/p/global-payments-still-trades-the)on Global Payments Inc. on The Mispricing Desk's Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls' thesis on GPN. Global Payments Inc.'s share was trading at $67.71 as of June 12th. GPN's trailing and forward P/E were 24.89 and 4.91 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. DLocal (DLO) Climbs 9.4% as Revenues Break Past $1 Billion Global Payments Inc. provides payment technology and software solutions for card, check, and digital-based payments in the Americas and internationally. GPN is emerging as a compelling capital-return and earnings-recovery story following the completion of its Worldpay-related portfolio restructuring. While the market continues to focus on integration complexity, acquisition- accounting charges, amortization expenses, and the company's leveraged balance sheet, the underlying business is demonstrating resilient operating performance and significant shareholder value creation. **Read More:**[**15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors Rich**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/15-ai-stocks-that-are-quietly- making-investors-rich-1714967/) **Read More:** [**Undervalued AI Stock Poised For Massive Gains: 10000% Upside Potential**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/undervalued-ai-stock-poised- for-massive-gains-10000-upside-19/) In the first quarter of 2026, Global Payments reported 5.5% normalized adjusted net revenue growth, adjusted operating margins of 42.4%, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.96, while reaffirming full-year adjusted EPS guidance of $13.80 to $14.00 and adjusted net revenue growth of 5% to 6%. More importantly, management has aggressively deployed capital into share repurchases, retiri

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] claims GPN is 37.6% undervalued relative to a narrative fair value of $107.16, citing recent revenue and net income growth despite declining share price returns. [2] notes Susquehanna maintains a Positive rating with a $111 price target (77% upside), but TD Cowen lowered its target to $74 (19% upside) with a Hold rating, citing cautious near-term headwinds. [3] highlights a bullish thesis focusing on capital returns and earnings recovery post-Worldpay restructuring, with Q1 2026 normalized adjusted net revenue growth of 5.5% and reaffirmed full-year adjusted EPS guidance of $13.80–$14.00.

- **Why it can work:** The stock trades at a forward P/E of 3.98 and price-to-book of 0.74, suggesting deep value if earnings normalize. The RSI acceleration score of 100.0 and RSI delta of +16.1 indicate a sharp momentum shift from prior weakness, potentially signaling a reversal. The bullish thesis in [3] points to aggressive share repurchases and resilient operating margins (42.4% adjusted), which could drive EPS growth even if revenue growth is modest.

- **What can break it:** The stock is down 8.4% in the past month and 26.1% from its 52-week high, underperforming its sector median return of +0.3%. [2] highlights TD Cowen’s cautious outlook on previously disclosed headwinds that now appear more likely, and Susquehanna reduced growth assumptions to 3.2% for Q2 2025. The sentiment score is low at 0.2, and short interest is 5.3% of float, indicating bearish positioning. Leverage from the Worldpay restructuring remains a risk, as noted in [3].

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Monitor Q2 2025 earnings for actual normalized adjusted net revenue growth versus Susquehanna’s reduced 3.2% estimate. Watch for debt reduction or free cash flow improvements to address balance sheet concerns. Track insider buying or further analyst upgrades/downgrades. A sustained move above the 52-week low of ~$62 (from which it is only 9.5% above) would be a technical confirmation.

- **Bottom line:** GPN shows a compelling valuation (forward P/E ~4, P/B <1) and a sharp RSI acceleration, but near-term headwinds and weak price momentum (down 8.4% in 1 month) create uncertainty. The bullish case rests on earnings recovery and capital returns, while the bear case focuses on execution risk and leverage. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Pzena Focused Value Strategy Added Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (CTSH) Ignoring Overblown AI Risk
sources.excerpt: [Pzena Investment Management](https://www.insidermonkey.com/hedge-fund/pzena- investment-management/435/) recently released its first-quarter 2026 commentary for "Pzena Focused Value Strategy." A copy of the letter can be [downloaded here](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/pzena-focused-value- strategys-q1-2026-investor-letter-1787460/). The equity market faced challenges in the first quarter due to the Iran conflict and the related energy price hike, compounded by continued AI-related uncertainty. Despite this volatility, energy stocks led the value index higher, but the portfolio fell short of the index and returned -4.7% (net) vs. 2.1% for the Russell 1000 Value Index. Despite the uncertainty, the firm believes that the current environment presents a strong long-term opportunity. In addition, you can check the Fund's top five holdings to see its best picks for 2026. In its first-quarter 2026 investor letter, Pzena Focused Value Strategy highlighted Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:[CTSH](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CTSH)). Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) is a professional services company that offers consulting and technology, and outsourcing services. On June 22, 2026, Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) closed at $41.83 per share. One-month return of Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) was -19.78%, and its shares lost 46.23% over the past 52 weeks. Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) has a market capitalization of $19.79 billion. Pzena Focused Value Strategy stated the following regarding Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) in its Q1 2026 investor letter: > "The health care, financials, and technology sectors were the largest > detract

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Pzena Focused Value Strategy added CTSH in Q1 2026, calling the AI risk "overblown" and viewing the current environment as a "strong long-term opportunity" [1]. The fund noted CTSH fell 46.23% over 52 weeks and was a top detractor due to health care, financials, and technology sector weakness [1]. No other external sources were provided; management or analyst commentary beyond this is unknown.

- **Why it can work**: CTSH trades at a forward P/E of 8.59 and a PEG of 0.87, suggesting the market is pricing in low growth expectations. The RSI acceleration from -3.6 to +11.2 and a 12.7% short float indicate potential for a short squeeze if sentiment shifts. Pzena's thesis that AI disruption risk is overblown implies current valuation may already discount worst-case scenarios, leaving room for recovery if earnings stabilize.

- **What can break it**: The stock is down 52.7% from its 52-week high and 21.4% in the past month, with a sentiment score of only 0.2 (very negative). The 1-month peer lag of 15% suggests CTSH is underperforming its sector materially. If AI-driven demand shifts permanently away from legacy IT services, or if macro headwinds (e.g., energy price hikes, Iran conflict) persist, earnings could deteriorate further, making the low P/E a value trap.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Look for stabilization in revenue growth or margin guidance in the next earnings report, a reduction in the short float (currently 12.7%), and any positive analyst revisions to forward estimates. Also monitor sector-relative performance: if CTSH stops lagging peers (peer lag >15% is extreme), it may signal a turning point.

- **Bottom line**: CTSH is a deeply out-of-favor value play with a low forward P/E and high short interest, supported by one value-oriented fund's contrarian bet. However, the extreme price decline, negative sentiment, and lack of positive catalysts from provided sources mean the setup is speculative. Without evidence of a fundamental turnaround, the long thesis relies heavily on mean reversion and the "overblown AI risk" narrative, which is unproven.

sources.title: Kroger's Board of Directors Raises Quarterly Dividend by 11%
sources.excerpt: CINCINNATI, June 25, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Kroger Co.'s (NYSE: KR) Board of Directors approved a dividend increase from $1.40 to $1.56 per year. The next quarterly dividend of 39 cents per share will be paid on September 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of close of business on August 15, 2026. The Kroger Co. Logo The company's quarterly dividend has grown at a 13% compounded annual growth rate since it was reinstated in 2006. This marks the 20th consecutive year of dividend increases. The company continues to expect, subject to board approval, an increasing dividend over time. "This dividend increase reflects the Board of Directors' confidence in Kroger's operating performance, durable free cash flow generation, and commitment to deliver long-term value for shareholders," said Ron Sargent, Chairman of Kroger's Board of Directors. Kroger remains committed to balanced capital allocation by investing in the business to drive sustainable growth, maintaining its current investment grade debt rating and returning capital to shareholders. **About Kroger** The [Kroger](https://www.kroger.com/) Co. (NYSE: KR) is one of America's largest retailers, serving more than 11 million customers daily through a digital shopping experience and retail food stores under a variety of [banner names](https://www.thekrogerco.com/about/our-business/). With more than 400,000 associates across our family of companies, Kroger is committed to providing America with affordable, great-tasting food and creating #ZeroHungerZeroWaste communities. To learn more about us, visit our [newsroom](https://ir.kroger.com/news/default.aspx) and [investor relations](https://edge.prnewswire.com/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4717663-1&h=313921986&u=https%3A%2F%2Fir.kroger.com%2Foverview%2Fdefault.aspx&a=investor+relation

sources.title: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From Kroger’s Q1 Earnings Call
sources.excerpt: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From Kroger's Q1 Earnings Call Kroger's first quarter saw a negative market reaction as the company's profit missed Wall Street's expectations, despite revenue exceeding consensus and ongoing growth in key areas. Management pointed to rising operating costs outpacing sales and highlighted uneven execution across stores as meaningful challenges. CEO Greg Foran described the current pace of cost growth as "not sustainable, and frankly, it's not acceptable," emphasizing efforts to close operational gaps and deliver more consistent performance. The company also noted a shift in customer behavior toward more deliberate, value-driven shopping patterns due to inflation and reduced government benefits. Is now the time to buy KR? [Find out in our full research report (it's free).](https://stockstory.org/us/stocks/nyse/kr?utm_source=earningsCall&utm_medium=yahoo&utm_campaign=earningsCallIntroCTA&utm_article=Clpxb2MOZEQ%3D&utm_ticker=KR) ### Kroger (KR) Q1 CY2026 Highlights: * **Revenue:** $46.12 billion vs analyst estimates of $45.46 billion (2.2% year-on-year growth, 1.4% beat) * **EPS (GAAP):** $1.47 vs analyst expectations of $1.60 (8% miss) * **EPS (GAAP) guidance for the full year** is $5.20 at the midpoint, beating analyst estimates by 1.2% * **Operating Margin:** 3.1%, in line with the same quarter last year * **Locations:** 2,828.5 at quarter end, up from 2,789.5 in the same quarter last year * **Same-Store Sales** rose 1% year on year (3.2% in the same quarter last year) * **Market Capitalization:** $39.28 billion While we enjoy listening to the management's commentary, our favorite part of earnings calls is the analyst questions. Those are unscripted and can often highlight topics that management teams would rather avoid or topic

sources.title: Is KR Stock a Buy Now or a Value Trap for Cautious Investors?
sources.excerpt: **The Kroger Co.** KR looks inexpensive, but cheap is not the same as risk- free. The stock's investment case depends on whether low expectations already reflect the pressures facing the grocery chain. KR offers valuation support, cash returns and balance-sheet flexibility. At the same time, softer consumer demand, pharmacy headwinds and margin pressure keep the bullish case from becoming more forceful. ## Why KR Looks Cheap on Earnings Kroger trades at 10.58X forward 12-month earnings, well below the Zacks sub- industry at 35.69X, the broader sector at 22.42X and the S&P 500 at 21.32X. That discount gives value investors a clear reason to keep KR on the watchlist. Still, the valuation case is not built around a sharp re-rating. The $60 price target reflects 11.21X forward 12-month earnings, pointing to modest upside rather than a call for the market to assign Kroger a much higher multiple. Zacks Investment Research Image Source: Zacks Investment Research ## How Kroger Supports Shareholder Returns Kroger's balance sheet adds credibility to the value argument. Net total debt to adjusted EBITDA stood at 1.75 at quarter-end, below the company's target range of 2.30 to 2.50, giving management room to invest while preserving financial flexibility. Capital returns also remain part of the story. Kroger pays a dividend and has a $2 billion share repurchase authorization approved in December 2025, which it expects to complete by the end of fiscal 2026. Management's long-term model targets sustainable total shareholder return of 8-11%, supported by earnings growth, identical sales growth, margin expansion and cash payout. ## What Is Keeping KR From a Stronger Call The main concern is that Kroger's low multiple reflects real operating pressure. Gross margin was 22.7% in the first

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Kroger’s board raised the dividend 11% (to $1.56/year), marking the 20th consecutive annual increase, citing confidence in operating performance and durable free cash flow [1]. However, Q1 earnings showed a GAAP EPS miss of 8% versus consensus, with management flagging “not sustainable” cost growth and uneven store execution [2]. The stock trades at 10.6x forward earnings, a deep discount to the sector (22.4x) and S&P 500 (21.3x), but the $60 price target implies only modest upside [3].

- **Why it can work**: The dividend increase signals board-level confidence in cash flow generation, and the net-debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 1.75x is well below the 2.3–2.5x target, providing balance-sheet flexibility [1][3]. A $2 billion buyback authorization (expected to be completed by end of fiscal 2026) and a long-term total shareholder return target of 8–11% support a capital-return narrative [3]. The forward P/E of 11.2x and PEG of 0.61 suggest the market is pricing in low growth, leaving room for upside if execution improves.

- **What can break it**: The Q1 EPS miss was driven by rising operating costs that management itself called “not sustainable” [2]. Gross margin of 22.7% and same-store sales growth of just 1% (down from 3.2% a year ago) indicate top-line deceleration and margin pressure [2][3]. Consumer behavior is shifting to more value-driven, deliberate shopping due to inflation and reduced benefits, which could further pressure revenue and margins [2]. The stock is 24% off its 52-week high and has underperformed its sector by 13% in the past month, suggesting negative momentum that may persist if cost headwinds continue.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor upcoming quarterly reports for evidence that cost growth is decelerating and that management’s operational gap-closing efforts are yielding measurable margin improvement. Watch for same-store sales trends to stabilize or accelerate above 1%, and for gross margin to hold or expand from 22.7%. Track the pace of the $2 billion buyback and any further dividend increases as signals of sustained free cash flow. Also watch for analyst revisions to forward EPS estimates—the current full-year guidance of $5.20 beat consensus by only 1.2%, so any downgrade would weaken the value case.

- **Bottom line**: Kroger offers a compelling capital-return story (dividend growth, buyback, low leverage) at a deep valuation discount, but the Q1 earnings miss and management’s own admission of “not sustainable” cost growth introduce real operational risk. The long setup depends on cost control and stabilization of same-store sales—neither of which is confirmed by the available data. Without evidence of margin recovery, the low multiple may reflect structural headwinds rather than a mispricing opportunity. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: TG Therapeutics (TGTX) Soars 7.5%: Is Further Upside Left in the Stock?
sources.excerpt: **TG Therapeutics** TGTX shares ended the last trading session 7.5% higher at $46.62. The jump came on an impressive volume with a higher-than-average number of shares changing hands in the session. This compares to the stock's 2% gain over the past four weeks. TG Therapeutics scored a strong price increase, on investors' optimism driven by the recent announcement of positive topline Phase 1 data for BRIUMVI in patients with myasthenia gravis (MG). The company also initiated the Phase 2 clinical trial evaluating BRIUMVI as a maintenance therapy following induction with efgartigimod in adult patients with MG. This biopharmaceutical company is expected to post quarterly earnings of $0.42 per share in its upcoming report, which represents a year-over-year change of +147.1%. Revenues are expected to be $230.64 million, up 63.4% from the year- ago quarter. Earnings and revenue growth expectations certainly give a good sense of the potential strength in a stock, but empirical research shows that trends in earnings estimate revisions are strongly correlated with near-term stock price movements. For TG Therapeutics, the consensus EPS estimate for the quarter has been revised 10.6% higher over the last 30 days to the current level. And a positive trend in earnings estimate revision usually translates into price appreciation. So, make sure to keep an eye on TGTX going forward to see if this recent jump can turn into more strength down the road. The stock currently carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today's Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) stocks here >>>> TG Therapeutics belongs to the Zacks Medical - Biomedical and Genetics industry. Another stock from the same industry, **Nurix Therapeutics, Inc.** NRIX, closed the last trading session 2.2% lower at $15.

Capitalize on the AI infrastructure supercycle with our selection of the [48 best 'picks and shovels' of the AI gold rush](https://simplywall.st/discover/investing-ideas/473744/ai-infrastructure- stocks/global?utm_medium=finance_user&utm_campaign=intro&utm_source=yahoo&blueprint=4619621) converting record-breaking demand into massive cash flow. ## TG Therapeutics Investment Narrative Recap To own TG Therapeutics, you need to be comfortable with a largely single‑product story around BRIUMVI and the company's effort to defend and expand that franchise. The Phase 1 subcutaneous BRIUMVI data look supportive of the key near‑term catalyst, the Phase 3 readout expected in late 2026 or early 2027, while also helping address the risk that payers and patients push more aggressively toward self‑administered therapies. The biggest ongoing risk remains concentration in one MS asset. Among recent announcements, the Phase 3 ENHANCE trial showing that a single 600 mg Day 1 infusion is bioequivalent to the current two‑step IV initiation is particularly releva

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] highlights a bullish thesis on TGTX as a commercial-stage biopharma with durable BRIUMVI revenue, growing prescription adoption, and patient persistence. [2] notes positive Phase 1 data for BRIUMVI in myasthenia gravis (MG) and a 10.6% upward revision in consensus EPS estimates over 30 days. [3] emphasizes positive Phase 1 results for an at-home subcutaneous BRIUMVI formulation, with a Phase 3 readout expected in late 2026 or early 2027, but warns of concentration risk in a single MS asset.

- **Why it can work:** BRIUMVI’s expanding commercial footprint and potential for at-home self-administration (via subcutaneous autoinjector) could drive adoption and patient retention, as noted in [1] and [3]. Upcoming Phase 3 data for subcutaneous dosing and positive early MG trial results [2] offer pipeline optionality beyond MS. Earnings and revenue growth expectations are strong (+147% and +63% YoY, respectively), with upward estimate revisions [2] supporting near-term momentum.

- **What can break it:** The company remains a single-product story (BRIUMVI) [3], making it vulnerable to competitive pressure, regulatory setbacks, or slower-than-expected prescription growth. The Phase 3 subcutaneous readout is not until late 2026/early 2027 [3], leaving a long gap without major catalysts. High short interest (18.4% of float) signals bearish sentiment, and the stock is near its 52-week high (-7%), increasing downside risk if momentum fades.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Monitor quarterly prescription data for BRIUMVI and revenue beats vs. consensus (next report expected with EPS of $0.42 on $230.64M revenue) [2]. Watch for updates on the Phase 2 MG trial and any early Phase 3 subcutaneous data releases. Sustained upward earnings estimate revisions and declining short interest would support the thesis.

- **Bottom line:** TGTX has a strong commercial product with pipeline catalysts, but the single-asset risk and long wait for key Phase 3 data create uncertainty. The high short interest and near-52-week-high price suggest caution; the setup is speculative and dependent on execution. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM) Moves 9.3% Higher: Will This Strength Last?
sources.excerpt: **Allegro MicroSystems, Inc.** ALGM shares ended the last trading session 9.3% higher at $59. The jump came on an impressive volume with a higher-than- average number of shares changing hands in the session. This compares to the stock's 21.8% gain over the past four weeks. The optimism surrounding the stock can be attributed to strong growth in Allegro MicroSystems' automotive business. The company is benefiting from rising adoption of its sensing and power solutions in electric vehicles, ADAS applications, high-voltage traction inverters, steering and braking systems, which drove 30% growth in its Focus Auto business in fiscal 2026. Further, strong design win momentum in China ADAS applications and increasing dollar content per vehicle are supporting investor confidence in the company's long- term growth outlook. This company is expected to post quarterly earnings of $0.21 per share in its upcoming report, which represents a year-over-year change of +133.3%. Revenues are expected to be $252.99 million, up 24.4% from the year-ago quarter. Earnings and revenue growth expectations certainly give a good sense of the potential strength in a stock, but empirical research shows that trends in earnings estimate revisions are strongly correlated with near-term stock price movements. For Allegro MicroSystems, the consensus EPS estimate for the quarter has remained unchanged over the last 30 days. And a stock's price usually doesn't keep moving higher in the absence of any trend in earnings estimate revisions. So, make sure to keep an eye on ALGM going forward to see if this recent jump can turn into more strength down the road. The stock currently carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see **the complete list of today's Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) stocks here >>>>** Allegro Micro

This points to a stock where optimism about growth potential and risk appetite appear elevated. If this kind of move has your attention, it could be a good moment to see what else is gaining traction in AI hardware and infrastructure through the [48 AI infrastructure stocks](https://simplywall.st/discover/investing- ideas/473744/ai-infrastructure- stocks/global?utm_medium=finance_user&utm_campaign=cta_screener_ai_infrastructure&utm_source=yahoo&blueprint=4623458) With Allegro MicroSystems posting strong recent returns and trading only about 8% below one set of analyst targets, the key question is simple: is the stock still undervalued, or is the market already pricing in its future growth? ## Most Popular Narrative: 7.4% Undervalued Analysts' most followed narrative pegs Allegro MicroSystems' fair value at about $54.42, a touch above the last close

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] attributes the recent 9.3% single-day and 21.8% four-week gains to strong growth in Allegro’s automotive business, specifically electric vehicles, ADAS, traction inverters, and steering/braking systems, with 30% growth in its Focus Auto business in fiscal 2026. [2] notes the stock is trading only about 8% below one set of analyst targets, with a fair value estimate of ~$54.42, implying it is near that target. [1] also states the consensus EPS estimate for the upcoming quarter has not changed in the last 30 days, which the source views as a cautionary signal for sustained price momentum.

- **Why it can work:** The company is benefiting from secular trends in vehicle electrification and ADAS, with rising dollar content per vehicle and strong design win momentum in China ADAS applications [1]. The 30% growth in the Focus Auto business and expected 133.3% year-over-year EPS growth in the upcoming quarter suggest a fundamental catalyst that could support further upside if realized [1]. The stock’s 20.6% one-month return and RSI acceleration of 7.5 (from a prior negative average) indicate improving short-term momentum, which could attract trend-following capital.

- **What can break it:** The stock is trading at a forward P/E of 32.72 and price-to-book of 9.36, which are elevated relative to typical value thresholds. [2] explicitly questions whether the market is already pricing in future growth, noting the stock is only ~8% below analyst targets. The lack of upward EPS estimate revisions over the last 30 days, despite the price surge, is a potential divergence that [1] flags as a risk to sustained momentum. Additionally, the short interest of 6.4% of float could lead to a squeeze, but if the fundamental narrative disappoints, short sellers may not be forced to cover. The company’s reliance on automotive cyclicality and China-specific demand introduces macro and geopolitical unknowns not covered in the excerpts.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Upward revisions to consensus EPS estimates for the upcoming quarter or fiscal year would strengthen the fundamental case, as [1] notes that price moves without estimate revisions are less sustainable. Positive pre-announcements or guidance raises from the company, especially regarding automotive order trends or design win conversions, would be key. A sustained increase in trading volume on up days, beyond the “higher-than-average” volume noted in [1], would support institutional accumulation. Any commentary from the company on China ADAS market share or dollar content per vehicle growth would help validate the narrative.

- **Bottom line:** The long setup is supported by a strong automotive electrification/ADAS narrative and recent price momentum, but the valuation is already near analyst fair value estimates, and earnings estimates have not been revised upward despite the rally. The stock appears to be pricing in optimistic growth assumptions that must be confirmed by actual earnings beats and guidance raises. Without those confirmations, the risk of mean reversion is elevated. No trade recommendation is made.

sources.title: Why Is Match Group (MTCH) Down 9.7% Since Last Earnings Report?
sources.excerpt: It has been about a month since the last earnings report for Match Group (MTCH). Shares have lost about 9.7% in that time frame, underperforming the S&P 500. Will the recent negative trend continue leading up to its next earnings release, or is Match Group due for a breakout? Well, first let's take a quick look at its latest earnings report in order to get a better handle on the recent drivers for Match Group Inc. before we dive into how investors and analysts have reacted as of late. ### MTCH Q1 Earnings & Revenues Beat Estimates, Sales Nearly Flat Y/Y Match Group reported first-quarter 2026 earnings of 95 cents per share, which surpassed the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 3.26%. The bottom line grew 41.8% from the year-ago quarter's reported figure. Revenues were $864 million, which rose 3.9% year over year and beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 1.06%. On an FX-neutral basis, revenues were flat year over year at $832.3 million. Direct revenues were $848 million, up 4.4% year over year, while indirect revenues declined 14.3% to $16 million. Top-line growth was primarily driven by strength at Hinge, where direct revenues rose 28.3% year over year, and was aided by a one-time benefit from Canada's rescission of its digital services tax. ### MTCH's Quarterly Details In the first quarter, the total number of payers decreased 5% year over year to 13.5 million. The figure missed the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 0.86%. Total revenues per payer (RPP) increased 10% year over year to $20.9. The figure beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 2.13%. Direct revenues from Tinder were up 2% year over year to $455 million (down 3% on an FX-neutral basis). The figure surpassed the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 3.17%. Tinder RPP rose 7% year over year to $17.56, and payers declined 5% to 8.6 m

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Source [1] notes a “most popular narrative” among Simply Wall St users that MTCH is 60% overvalued, with a user-derived fair value of $34.51—essentially flat to the current price. The same source also cites an analyst price target of $41.06 and an estimated intrinsic discount of ~55%, creating a wide gap between crowd sentiment and formal valuation models. Source [2] reports that MTCH beat Q1 2026 earnings and revenue estimates, but revenues were flat year-over-year on an FX-neutral basis, and total payers declined 5% to 13.5 million. Tinder direct revenues were up only 2% (down 3% FX-neutral), while Hinge direct revenues grew 28.3%.

- **Why it can work**: The stock trades at a forward P/E of 8.07 and a PEG of 0.31, suggesting the market is pricing in minimal growth or a decline. If Hinge’s 28.3% direct revenue growth continues to offset Tinder’s payer erosion, and if the analyst price target of $41.06 (18% above current) is credible, the current valuation may already discount the worst. The RSI acceleration from -1.5 to +1.7 and the stock’s relative outperformance vs. its sector (-1.3% vs. -10.5% median) could indicate early momentum stabilization.

- **What can break it**: The dominant user narrative in [1] is that MTCH is overvalued at current levels, implying limited upside without a catalyst. Payer counts are shrinking (-5% overall, -5% at Tinder), and Tinder’s FX-neutral revenue decline suggests core brand weakness. The 1-month return is still negative (-1.3%), and the stock is down 9.7% since the last earnings report [2]. If Hinge growth decelerates or Tinder payers continue to fall, the low forward P/E may prove to be a value trap rather than a discount.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor next quarter’s payer trends—specifically whether Tinder’s payer decline stabilizes and whether Hinge can maintain >25% direct revenue growth. Also watch for any upward revision to analyst price targets or a shift in the Simply Wall St crowd narrative away from “60% overvalued.” A sustained move above the 50-day moving average (not provided) and a positive sentiment score (currently -0.2) would be supportive.

- **Bottom line**: MTCH presents a mixed long setup: cheap on forward earnings and PEG, with a strong sub-brand (Hinge) and a beaten-down price. However, the primary qualitative input from [1] shows the most-followed crowd view is that the stock is fairly valued to overvalued, and [2] confirms payer erosion and flat FX-neutral revenue. The long thesis depends entirely on Hinge’s growth re-accelerating overall revenue and reversing payer declines—neither of which is confirmed by the supplied data. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Is Galaxy Digital (GLXY) A Good Stock To Buy Now?

GLXY's trailing and forward P/E were 47.25 and 666.67 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. Galaxy Digital Inc. (GLXY) is positioned as a differentiated infrastructure and financial services platform at the intersection of two of the most powerful global megatrends: artificial intelligence and digital assets. The investment case is increasingly supported by structural constraints in global AI expansion, where electricity availability has become the primary bottleneck rather than chip supply alone. Galaxy's strategically located and power-rich Texas data center footprint represents a sc

analysis:
- **External commentary:** The cited bullish thesis [1] frames Galaxy Digital as a dual‑engine platform capturing both institutional crypto adoption and AI compute demand, with its Texas data centers positioned to benefit from electricity‑supply constraints that are now the primary bottleneck for AI expansion. The source notes a forward P/E of 666.67 as of June 12, implying extreme earnings expectations. No source excerpts discuss management quality, balance sheet risks, or regulatory exposure.

- **Why it can work:** The structural thesis [1] suggests Galaxy’s infrastructure segment could monetize the AI‑driven need for power‑rich data centers, while its digital‑assets platform may gain from rising institutional crypto activity. If both megatrends accelerate simultaneously, the company could see revenue growth from two uncorrelated drivers. The current RSI of 42.4 and 1‑month return of +2.5% (lagging the sector median of +5.9%) may indicate a pullback that could re‑rate if catalysts emerge.

- **What can break it:** The forward P/E of 326.07 implies the market already prices in substantial future growth; any disappointment in AI data‑center utilization or crypto trading volumes could trigger a sharp de‑rating. The stock is 33.9% below its 52‑week high, suggesting prior optimism faded. Unknown: Galaxy’s actual data‑center power contracts, utilization rates, and the sustainability of its crypto revenue streams. Regulatory or competitive shifts in either AI infrastructure or digital assets are not addressed in the excerpts.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for quarterly updates on data‑center occupancy, power‑purchase agreements, and AI client additions. Also monitor Galaxy’s digital‑assets trading and lending volumes, as well as Bitcoin/Ether price trends, since crypto exposure is a key driver. Any insider buying or analyst upgrades would be incremental, but no such data is provided.

- **Bottom line:** The long setup rests on a plausible but unverified dual‑megatrend narrative [1] at a very high valuation. The warehouse metrics show a pullback in a lagging stock (RSI 42.4, 1‑month return below sector median), but the opportunity score of 79.7 is not supported by fundamental data in the excerpts. Without evidence of execution or margin trends, the thesis remains speculative. No trade recommendation.

sources.excerpt: Bio-Techne (TECH) has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $65.33, according to Upgrade to read this MT Newswires article and get so much more. A Silver or Gold subscription plan is required to access premium news articles.

analysis:
- **External commentary:** Source [2] reports that Bio-Techne is to be acquired by Merck for $11.3 billion, which would represent a significant premium. Source [3] notes a Leerink downgrade to Market Perform with a $73 price target, and the mean analyst target is $65.33. Source [1] is a generic market update with no specific Bio-Techne commentary. The acquisition news is the primary qualitative input; the downgrade and mean target suggest some analysts see limited upside at current levels.

- **Why it can work:** The announced acquisition by Merck [2] provides a clear, binding catalyst for the stock to converge toward the deal price, reducing downside risk. The current price of $70.55 is above the mean analyst target of $65.33 [3], implying the market is already pricing in a premium. If the deal closes as announced, the stock could rise to the acquisition price, offering a potential arbitrage spread. The RSI acceleration (delta 9.2) and 1-month return of +22.1% suggest momentum is aligning with the deal news.

- **What can break it:** The acquisition may face regulatory hurdles, shareholder opposition, or a competing bid that falls through, causing the stock to revert to pre-deal levels. The Leerink downgrade [3] and mean target of $65.33 indicate that without the deal, the stock is overvalued. The trailing P/E of 75.21 and forward P/E of 25.54 suggest high earnings expectations that may not be met if the deal fails. Short interest of 8.8% could amplify downside if the deal collapses.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Regulatory filings (e.g., Hart-Scott-Rodino clearance), shareholder votes, and any updates on the Merck acquisition timeline. Monitor the spread between the current price and the reported $11.3 billion deal price (implied per-share value unknown from excerpts). Also watch for any analyst upgrades or downgrades post-announcement, and the stock’s ability to hold above the mean target of $65.33 [3].

- **Bottom line:** The long setup is entirely dependent on the successful closure of the Merck acquisition [2]. The stock has already rallied 22.1% in the past month, likely reflecting deal anticipation, and trades above the mean analyst target. The primary risk is deal failure, which could lead to a sharp reversion. Without the deal, fundamentals (high trailing P/E, low value scores) do not support the current price. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: TTEK Clinches Single-Award Contract From Encina Wastewater Authority
sources.excerpt: **Tetra Tech, Inc.** TTEK recently announced that it has secured a single- award deal from Encina Wastewater Authority (EWA). The latest deal will involve the company providing digital automation and operational technology network solutions for EWA's Cogeneration Low-Emission Augmentation and Retrofit (CLEAR) project in California. As part of the deal, Tetra Tech will help in installing microgrid control and gas conditioning systems within the digital infrastructure of the facility. TTEK's digital systems professionals will work on integrating information systems across biological trickling filters, oxidation catalyst emissions controls, a digester biogas conditioning facility, a 1-megawatt linear generator facility and a 1.5 megawatt-hour battery energy storage system. This will improve the wastewater treatment process by enhancing the biogas efficiency and reducing emissions, thereby supporting EWA in offering reliable wastewater treatment and resource recovery services. This deal is a testimony to the long-standing collaboration between both the entities. It's worth noting that TTEK has been providing operational technology and systems integration services since 2017. Lately, Tetra Tech has received a series of deals that are likely to drive its growth. In June 2026, TTEK secured a contract from Chelan County Public Utility District ("PUD") for the Hydropower Dam Spillway Modernization Project at the Rock Island and Rocky Reach hydroelectric dams in Washington. The company will serve as the lead design engineer as part of a team that includes Kuney Construction and J.F. Brennan. Also, it recently secured an engineering and technical consultancy framework from a Scotland-based company, Scotland Excel, to provide services for infrastructure and environmental projects a

sources.title: Tetra Tech (TTEK) Stock After Yearlong Slide Is Today’s Valuation Attractive

analysis:
- **External commentary**: [1] reports TTEK secured a single-award contract from Encina Wastewater Authority for digital automation and microgrid control systems, part of a cogeneration retrofit project in California. The excerpt also notes a series of recent deals, including a hydropower dam spillway modernization contract in Washington and an engineering framework in Scotland. [2] is garbled and provides no usable commentary. No source discusses valuation, earnings, or management guidance.

- **Why it can work**: The contract win [1] signals continued demand for TTEK’s digital and environmental engineering services, particularly in water infrastructure and emissions reduction. The stock is 33.8% below its 52-week high, with a forward P/E of 16.08 and a 1-month return of +3.4% versus a sector median of +0.3%, suggesting relative momentum. RSI acceleration from -1.5 to +8.2 indicates short-term buying pressure. The short float of 5.0% is moderate but not extreme.

- **What can break it**: The stock is still 33.8% below its 52-week high, implying persistent selling pressure or negative sentiment. The PEG ratio of 2.1 suggests the forward P/E is not cheap relative to expected growth. The sentiment score of 0.2 is neutral, not bullish. The garbled [2] source cannot confirm whether valuation is attractive. Unknown: order backlog, revenue visibility, margin trends, and whether recent contract wins are material to earnings.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Look for additional contract announcements or backlog updates in SEC filings. Monitor RSI to see if acceleration sustains above 60. Watch for analyst upgrades or earnings beats that could shift sentiment. A decline in short interest or an increase in institutional ownership would be supportive. Unknown: any insider buying or share repurchase activity.

- **Bottom line**: TTEK shows a short-term momentum setup with a recent contract win [1] and improving RSI, but the stock remains well below its high and valuation is not obviously cheap (PEG 2.1). Without confirmed earnings catalysts or backlog growth, the long case relies on continued contract flow and sector tailwinds. No trade recommendation.
